programme.title: Walker, Texas Ranger

programme.title: CSI: Miami
programme.desc: A five-star hotel's pool boy is found dead in its grounds with a key to the penthouse in his pocket. It soon becomes clear he had relations with all three women staying in the suite, leaving the CSIs with no shortage of suspects. Crime drama, starring David Caruso, Adam Rodriguez and Khandi Alexander.(n)

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: The team's mission to track the arsonist targeting US Navy facilities appears to end in success when the man in question is killed by a thermite explosion on board the USS Brewer. Meanwhile, Abby studies evidence relating to fire-starter Bruce Johnson, who died when his car exploded, and discovers coded messages in his quarters that indicate he may have been working as part of a larger gang. Crime drama, starring Mark Harmon.(n)

programme.title: NCIS: Los Angeles
programme.desc: The team investigates when an Islamic terrorist group in Iraq steals a truck with $5million and track the money back to America. The agents learn the cash has been used to set up a bank robbery intended as a ruse to allow a bank employee to transfer $100million of the terrorists' money that has been frozen by the government in an offshore bank. Crime drama, starring Chris O'Donnell and LL Cool J.(n)

programme.title: Father Dowling Mysteries
programme.desc: Sister Steve gets drawn into a blind man's scheme to blackmail one of St Micheal's prominent parishioners. Drama, starring Tom Bosley as an American priest with a penchant for danger-filled investigations.(n)

programme.title: Perry Mason
programme.sub-title: The Case of the Red Riding Boots
programme.desc: On the eve of her wedding to a rancher, the body of a woman is found in a car boot and Perry must prove that a foreman accused of the murder is innocent.(n)

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: Tony tries to piece together the events that led to him lying wounded in a hospital bed, having been shot in the chest through his bulletproof vest. programme.title: Walker, Texas Ranger
programme.desc: A gang of hijackers are tracked down when one of Walker's friends disappears.(n)

programme.title: CSI: NY
programme.desc: Mac and Aiden try to solve the murder of a rap artist, who was stabbed after signing a recording contract and winning a DJ contest. Meanwhile, Stella and Danny search for the killer of a fashion designer found in her penthouse pool - a case that leads them to a sushi restaurant specialising in food served on nude models. With hip-hop star Master P.(n)

programme.title: NCIS: Los Angeles
programme.desc: Calvin Lee, an executive naval officer, appears to commit suicide by hanging himself from a public road bridge. When examining CCTV footage of the incident, Sam and Callen discover the dead man tossed away a small object before jumping to his death, and when they learn the late lieutenant commander had been working on a sensitive project involving new cloaking technology for submarines, they immediately take an interest in the item. The investigation draws the team into the furtive world of LA's Chinese community, where they discover Lee might have been harbouring some personal as well as professional secrets. Crime drama, starring Chris O'Donnell.(n)

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: The investigators examine the death of US marine Tommy Hill, who appears to have been fatally stabbed en route to a homecoming party thrown in his honour by his foster family. McGee and Ziva follow a trail of blood that leads them to a mobile phone, while Abby discovers the victim had been trying to hack into Virginia's state website and Ducky's autopsy reveals that Hill had been injected with a sedative.(n)

programme.title: CSI: Miami
programme.desc: Horatio becomes prime suspect in a murder investigation, and makes matters worse for himself by admitting he not only dated the victim, but was also the last person to see her alive. programme.title: NCIS: Los Angeles
programme.desc: A naval officer involved in an illegal underground street race is killed when his vehicle explodes. The team's investigations reveal he was carrying classified equipment in relation to his work on a new form of battery that the military had hoped to adopt, and that the explosion - captured on CCTV - is not in accordance with that expected of normal fuel. Meanwhile, Callen latest driving misdemeanour means he must attend traffic school. Chris O'Donnell stars.(n)

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: US Navy lieutenant Paul Booth is shot dead while sitting on a park bench. It turns out that the last people he called were his boss, his wife and Penelope Langston - a rebellious academic who also happens to be McGee's grandmother. She says that the victim was an old student of hers who kept in touch, but later reveals she was recruited to work on a special project during the Vietnam War by the head of a top-secret think tank Booth was a member of. Guest starring Lily Tomlin, with Mark Harmon and Sean Murray.(n)

programme.title: CSI: Miami

Gary Sinise guest stars.(n)

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: The team suspects a desperate US Marine may have faked his own death with the help of a friend and blown up an ambulance in a botched attempt to cover all traces of the subterfuge. Subsequent developments, including a mysterious disappearance, seem to confirm the hypothesis - until a timely discovery by Abby throws new light on the case.(n)

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: The agents are called to a national forest where several women have gone missing and conclude there may be a serial killer at large - a theory confirmed in gruesome fashion as the investigation proceeds. Drama, with Mark Harmon and David McCallum.(n)

programme.desc: The team is called to a high school on a marine base, where a pupil has taken his classmates hostage and is threatening to detonate a bomb strapped to his chest. programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: Gibbs is called to a frozen lake, where two children have discovered the body of a missing US Marine. The team searches the water for clues and finds three more bullet-ridden corpses, all of which have tattoos linking them to a notorious street gang. The case then becomes a race against time to discover what connects the murders before the serviceman's comrades seek revenge.(n)

Starring Gary Sinise, Melina Kanakaredes, Michele Hicks and Vanessa Ferlito.(n)

In this edition, Jay Aston, Mike Nolan, SuRie, Dana, Jade Ewen, John Lundvik, Niamh Kavanagh and Mans Zelmerlow take part in a special Eurovision edition of the general knowledge quiz. Plus, a special performance from Dana.(n)

programme.title: Eurovision: Come Together
programme.desc: With Eurovision 2020 unable to go ahead as planned, Graham Norton gives viewers the chance to vote for their all-time favourite Eurovision song from an eclectic shortlist, selected by a panel of experts. Graham also chats to James Newman, who was due to represent the UK in Rotterdam this year. There are previews of some of the other most talked-about acts who were due to take to the stage this year, and Eurovision fans and recreate classic moments in their homes. Former UK act Sonia even takes on the Epic Sax Guy solo that became so infamous after the 2010 Eurovision Song Contest.(n)

programme.title: Eurovision: Europe Shine a Light
programme.desc: A special programme honouring all 41 songs that would have competed in the 2020 Eurovision song contest. Just like the contest itself, the live show will be simultaneously broadcast across Europe and beyond, and will be hosted from the Netherlands by Dutch presenters Chantal Janzen, Edsilia Rombley and Jan Smit. There will be a virtual singalong, where fans from across the world will dress up and form a huge Eurovision choir to perform Johnny Logan's winning song for Ireland in 1980, What's Another Year. Graham Norton provides live commentary.(n)

This time they'll have two builders and two decorators to manage, and hope they'll do enough to impress judges Kelly Hoppen and Daniel Hopwood.(n)

programme.desc: The finalists are challenged to produce a three-course wedding menu to be taste-tested by the manager's family and friends. However, the enormity of the task takes its toll, resulting in a burnt main course and undercooked pear tart, and owner Sam has a difficult decision to make before one competitor is given the job of head chef.(n)

programme.title: Lorraine Pascale: How to Be a Better Cook
programme.desc: Neil Forster is an impatient cook who reaches for the takeaway menu rather than making food from scratch. Lorraine shows him recipes including asparagus and cream cheese puff pastry tarts, a quick way to do a roast chicken and a neat shortcut to prepare a summer fruits pudding.(n)

programme.title: The Pure Hell of St Trinian's (1960)
programme.desc: The girls burn their school to the ground, prompting a mysterious Middle Eastern gentleman to offer new premises for their studies - but he has a sinister ulterior motive for helping the badly behaved youngsters. Comedy, starring Cecil Parker, Joyce Grenfell, George Cole and Irene Handl.(n)

programme.title: Flog It!
programme.desc: Paul Martin presents from the Royal Marines Museum in Hampshire. Charles Hanson looks over a boxing glove signed Henry Cooper, and Catherine Southon learns about naval traditions.(n)

programme.title: Pointless
programme.desc: Quiz in which contestants try to score the fewest points possible by giving the least obvious correct answers to questions. Presented by Alexander Armstrong, with Richard Osman.(n)

programme.title: Richard Osman's House of Games
programme.desc: Comedian Chris Ramsey and broadcasters Anita Rani, Janet Ellis and Jamie Theakston continue the daily test of their general knowledge skills. Hosted by Richard Osman.(n)

programme.title: The Farmers' Country Showdown
programme.desc: Three farm managers, all critical to the running of their businesses, attend an agricultural awards ceremony in London, where they battle it out for Farm Manager of the Year.(n)

programme.title: Front Row Late
programme.desc: Mary asks why people turn to music in times of crisis, from free concerts streamed by A-list names to benefit singles, cross-balcony singalongs and quarantine choirs. She also asks whether the spirit of sharing and caring change the way we consume music in the long term, or whether the generosity will run out if and when we ‘get back to normal'.(n)

programme.title: The Lakes with Paul Rose
programme.desc: Remote Eskdale is the next destination in the explorer's trek through the Lake District, enjoying the landscapes and meeting its people. The corner of the western Lakes he focuses on is off the normal tourist track, yet boasts England's highest mountain. However, before making the tough climb to the summit of Scafell Pike, Paul meets the folk who run one of the country's most scenic railways, has a close encounter with a vulture, and gets to grips with the locals in a spot of Cumberland wrestling.(n)

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: The remaining regional finalists prepare their desserts, hoping to secure the final place on the banquet for the UK's finest children's authors and illustrators. The chefs serve up dishes inspired by a wide range of books from Matilda to Paddington Bear, with radio DJ Greg James joining the judges to choose their favourite.(n)

programme.title: Charlie Brooker's Antiviral Wipe
programme.desc: The writer and broadcaster returns to front his own show since the award-winning Charlie Brooker's 2016 Wipe, to take a satirical look at life under lockdown. As well as the crisis itself, he will be finding out what people have been watching to while away the hours, with help from the ever-insightful Philomena Cunk.(n)

programme.title: Cunk & Other Humans on 2019
programme.sub-title: Cunk & Other Humans on 2019: Jumbo All-in-One Edition
programme.desc: Philomena Cunk and other contributors take a look back at the major events of last year, including Brexit, climate change, Theresa May, Prince Andrew, Trump, Joker and Game of Thrones.(n)

programme.title: Live at the Apollo
programme.desc: Desiree Burch hosts the show recorded before a packed house at London's Hammersmith Apollo, warming up the audience and then introducing stand-up performances by Paul McCaffrey and Jonny Pelham.(n)

programme.title: Big Animal Surgery
programme.desc: Liz Bonnin travels to Tbilisi in Georgia where she watches an operation on an Asian elephant named Grande. Having survived a catastrophic flood at the zoo where he lives, Grande has a badly infected tusk which needs removing. But the vet is working against the clock as every minute Grande is under anaesthetic, the weight of his own body risks damaging his vital organs.(n)

programme.title: Growing Up Gifted
programme.desc: Part one of two. Return of the documentary following three gifted teenagers, Liam, Kian and Jamarley, as they prepare to finish secondary school and make important decisions about their futures. Kian dreams of becoming a theoretical physicist, but has to face up to the reality of having to leave Hartlepool to pursue his dreams. In Newcastle, aspiring doctor Liam finds his work ethic is increasingly under scrutiny, while in London, Jamarley faces pressures outside school.(n)

programme.title: The Hairy Bikers' Mediterranean Adventure
programme.desc: Si King and Dave Myers explore the culinary delights of southern Spain, travelling from Valencia to Torremolinos and finding out about the influence of the Moors, who invaded the country in the early eighth century. Along the way, they visit one of the most fertile areas in Spain, get their hands on a freshly caught octopus, go for a mud bath and learn about flamenco, giving Dave a chance to show off his dance moves. Finally, they cook tapas for the San Juan midsummer night festival, where they reflect upon their entire Mediterranean adventure.(n)

programme.title: Our Finest Hours
programme.desc: As the UK faces its biggest challenge since the Second World War, Sophie Raworth looks at how the nation has responded to adversity in the past and in the present. Using a combination of wartime archive and contemporary news footage, each episode looks at the impact of Covid-19 on families, communities and businesses, and compares how the nation has responded to crisis now and then.(n)

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two.(n)

programme.title: Our Finest Hours
programme.desc: Sophie Raworth reflects on how families, friends and neighbours have pulled together during the coronavirus lockdown, just as they did 80 years ago during the Second World War. As the crisis has worsened, the support for each other - and especially for NHS frontline staff - has strengthened. Different generations have all done their bit to keep themselves and those around then safe and to look after the most vulnerable members of the community.(n)

programme.title: Garden Rescue
programme.desc: Charlie Dimmock, Arit Anderson and Chelsea gold medal-winners Harry and David Rich help Kevin and Yvonne in Newbury to bring a touch of the exotic to their boring garden.(n)

programme.title: Inside the Factory
programme.desc: Gregg Wallace visits a liqueur factory in Ireland that produces 540,000 bottles a day. He tracks the journey of a bottle of cream liqueur through the three-year production timeline. At the other end of the journey, Cherry Healey discovers where the empty bottles are recycled. Plus, historian Ruth Goodman visits a monastery to discover the spiritual origins of liqueurs.(n)

programme.title: The Great Interior Design Challenge
programme.desc: In the second quarter-final from the coastal town of Aberaeron in Wales, the two designers battle it out as they are challenged to transform two rooms - a bedroom and a study/guest room - whilst incorporating rope as the creative challenge into their overall scheme. Elsewhere, architectural historian Tom Dyckhoff explores Aberaeron and uncovers its far-reaching architectural influences.(n)

programme.desc: No Details Available.(n)

programme.title: Lorraine Pascale: How to Be a Better Cook
programme.desc: Student Travis Francis admits his mum makes all his meals, but now he wants to cook for her. Lorraine creates a menu inspired by his family's Caribbean roots, including a prawn, sweet potato and mango curry and tempting chocolate and rum truffles. She also demonstrates a quick way to make bread.(n)

programme.title: Lorraine Pascale: How to Be a Better Cook
programme.desc: Sally Mack lacks the confidence to cook for anyone except herself, but after turning 50 she decided that had to change. Lorraine creates a Chinese-inspired menu of dishes that are simple to prepare, including sweet-and-sour ribs, chicken and cashew nut stir fry and a showstopping cake.(n)

programme.title: Eggheads
programme.desc: Jeremy Vine hosts the quiz in which the winners of famous game shows work as a team to tackle a new set of challengers hoping to win a cash prize.(n)

programme.title: Talking Pictures
programme.desc: A retrospective look at the television appearances made by actor Noel Coward, featuring archive interview clips showing the highlights and milestones of his life and career. Narrated by Sylvia Syms.(n)

programme.title: Brief Encounter (1945)
programme.desc: A suburban housewife meets a married doctor by chance in a railway station waiting room. Later, their paths cross many times until the pair become friends and their meetings a fixed arrangement. But as their feelings for each other transform from friendship into passion, both come to realise the affair is doomed to fail. David Lean's romantic drama, with Celia Johnson, Trevor Howard and Cyril Raymond.(n)

programme.title: Flog It!
programme.desc: Paul Martin touches down at historic hotspots such as Capesthorne Hall in Cheshire, where Anita Manning has a surprise in store for one lady. Paul also visits Chedworth Roman Villa.(n)

programme.title: Pointless
programme.desc: Quiz in which contestants try to score the fewest points possible by giving the least obvious correct answers to questions. Presented by Alexander Armstrong, with Richard Osman.(n)

programme.desc: Anita Rani, Chris Ramsey, Janet Ellis and Jamie Theakston compete in Double Points Friday, and their scores are tallied from the week resulting in an overall champion being crowned.(n)

programme.title: The Farmers' Country Showdown
programme.desc: The Akiki family, with its biodynamic fruit and vegetables and cheese-making clan the Spierings both hope to sell out of home-grown produce at one of London's oldest farmers' markets in Marylebone. A good day's trade will help the farms survive so every customer counts. Narrated by Denis Lawson.(n)

programme.title: Rick Stein's Road to Mexico
programme.desc: The chef lands in the town that placed Tequila on the map and explores Guadalajara, the city that gave the rest of the world Mariachis and dishes like carne con chilli. He also can't resist a visit to Casa Kimberly, bought by Richard Burton for Elizabeth Taylor, and where they still serve her favourite cocktail, a chocolate martini.(n)

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: The winning chefs prepare their courses at the banquet - a celebration of almost 200 years of British children's literature. Guests include Jacqueline Wilson, Cressida Cowell, Anthony Horowitz, Greg James, Francesca Simon and Charlie Higson, along with other leading children's authors and illustrators, as well as aspiring young writers, presenter Susan Calman and judges Andi Oliver, Matthew Fort and Oliver Peyton.(n)

programme.title: Gardeners' World
programme.desc: From her garden at Glebe Cottage in Devon, Carol Klein goes back to the basics of propagating plants by demonstrating how to take cuttings. There is also an interview with Advolly Richmond, whose love of garden history is reflected in her own garden, and she shares her passion for a flower with a surprising past. Joe Swift has a large plant in his London garden that is due for some timely remedial work, and the team travels to Brighton to visit a garden packed with exotic plants grown in raised beds and containers.(n)

programme.title: Later - with Jools Holland
programme.desc: The music programme returns with a lockdown twist. Each week, Jools chats to a guest via videolink, beginning with Christine & the Queens, who talks about her musical journey as well as her influences, with a selection of her favourite Later performances pulled out of the archive. New music will also continue to feature throughout the series, and in this first edition Laura Marling plays a track from her seventh album Song for Our Daughter, which she released earlier than originally planned in light of the current pandemic.(n)

programme.desc: Analysis of the day's events, presented by Kirsty Wark.(n)

programme.title: Pawn Sacrifice (2014)
programme.desc: American chess prodigy Bobby Fischer challenges the best player in the Soviet Union in a top championship, causing the two nations to use the event to fuel their respective agendas. Fischer soon finds himself caught between the two superpowers as well as having to deal with his own struggles. Tobey Maguire and Liev Schreiber star in this true-life sporting drama set in 1972 during the Cold War.(n)

programme.title: Panorama
programme.sub-title: Lockdown: How Business Is Coping - Panorama
programme.desc: Britain's economy has been turned upside down by the coronavirus crisis. Many companies are struggling to stay afloat, while some have found themselves swamped with unprecedented demand. Panorama follows some of the small business owners and key workers struggling through the lockdown - from the courier whose job it is to collect suspected samples of Covid-19, to the funeral director working round the clock to collect and bury the dead.(n)

programme.title: The Countess and the Russian Billionaire
programme.desc: An inside look at the secret world of Sergei Pugachev and his British partner Countess Alexandra Tolstoy. A hugely rich and powerful couple, with three beautiful children, homes across the world, and unimaginable wealth, the couple appeared to have it all, but their fairy tale life is about to come crashing down around them.(n)

programme.title: Our Finest Hours
programme.desc: Sophie Raworth reflects on how families, friends and neighbours have pulled together during the coronavirus lockdown, just as they did 80 years ago during the Second World War. As the crisis has worsened, the support for each other - and especially for NHS frontline staff - has strengthened. Different generations have all done their bit to keep themselves and those around then safe and to look after the most vulnerable members of the community.(n)

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two.(n)

programme.title: Wild & Weird
programme.desc: Naomi Wilkinson and Tim Warwood show more clips from the natural world, including an alien-like snail with pulsating antennae and a 10-metre long animal called a pyrosome.(n)

programme.title: All Over the Place
programme.desc: Ed Petrie and friends visit a secret nuclear bunker in Essex.(n)

programme.title: The Dengineers
programme.desc: Joe Tracini and Meryl Fernandes enlist expert designers and builders to construct dream dens for lucky youngsters.(n)

programme.title: Blue Peter
programme.sub-title: Blue Peter: Meet Our New Presenter!
programme.desc: Long-running children's magazine programme.(n)

programme.title: Deadly 60
programme.desc: Steve Backshall visits the southern states of America, where he spots a wild alligator and learns about the carnivorous Venus flytrap.(n)

programme.title: Deadly 60
programme.desc: Steve Backshall heads to Florida to track down America's largest snake, catch a slippery amphibian called the amphiuma and witness the hunting methods of bottlenose dolphins.(n)

programme.title: Astronauts: Do You Have What It Takes?
programme.desc: The finalists go to the shuttle landing facility on Florida's Space Coast, where they experience weightlessness for the first time, have their motor skills put to the test at 30,000 feet and are confronted with the dangers of space travel. They also undergo further training at a NASA underwater facility before Chris Hadfield reveals who the panel have chosen as the winner.(n)

programme.title: Ice Age Giants
programme.desc: Alice Roberts examines the latest discoveries from the northern hemisphere to shed light on the lives and habitats of the creatures that dominated the planet 40,000 years ago. She begins by heading to North America, which was home to a diverse group of giant mammals, and discovers how the sabre-toothed cat's out-sized teeth were vital for hunting, yet easily broken. Alice looks at evidence of the bear-sized Shasta ground sloth in a cave in the Grand Canyon, and learns about the strange armadillo-like glyptodont - which was the size of a small car.(n)

programme.title: Frankenweenie (2012)
programme.desc: A lonely boy uses electricity to bring his dead dog back to life. However, the presence of the animal in the neighbourhood leads to panic over a monster, while the young scientist's classmates are soon also meddling with life and death to revive their own pets. Tim Burton's stop-motion animated fantasy, with the voices of Charlie Tahan and Winona Ryder.(n)

programme.title: The Great British Sewing Bee
programme.desc: The amateur sewer competition hosted by Joe Lycett continues. This time, the nine remaining contestants take part in a series of challenges for sportswear week. To test the sewers' ability to create practical and stylish sportswear, judges Patrick Grant and Esme Young kick off the pattern challenge with a rugby shirt. In the transformation challenge, the contestants turn cagoules into miniature waterproof onesies for toddles, before taking on the difficult task of making tennis outfits in the made-to-measure challenge.(n)

programme.title: Hugh's Wild West
programme.desc: Hugh Fearnley-Whittingstall extensive wildlife tour continues as he visits Dorset, where the mild climate has provided all kinds of creatures with ideal habitat conditions. He meets retired policeman Mick Jenner, who has been filming an otter family for the past few years, and local bat enthusiast Colin Morris, who has been keeping a watchful eye on the rare Bechstein bat. Plus, at Portland Bird Observatory, Hugh joins ornithologist Martin Cade in monitoring migrating blackcaps and wheatears.(n)

programme.title: Talking Pictures
programme.desc: Looking at the Hollywood musical, with interviews from stars such as Fred Astaire and Julie Andrews, as well as discussion of films like Singin' in the Rain and The Sound of Music.(n)

programme.title: South Pacific (1958)
programme.desc: A naval nurse falls in love with a French plantation owner on an American-held island during the Second World War. However, dark secrets surface from his past and result in her having to leave the colony and embark on a perilous mission with the help of a US Navy officer. Romantic musical based on the Rodgers and Hammerstein Broadway show, with Mitzi Gaynor, Rossano Brazzi, John Kerr and Ray Walston.(n)

programme.title: Flog It!
programme.desc: Natasha Raskin Sharp and Raj Bisram value some Spiritualist photographs and a Hornby train set at Culzean Castle, while Paul Martin follows in the footsteps of Robert Burns.(n)

programme.title: John Le Mesurier: It's All Been Rather Lovely
programme.desc: A portrait of the prolific actor's life and career, from his turbulent marriage to Hattie Jacques to his renowned role as Sgt Wilson in the TV sitcom Dad's Army and parts in more than 100 films. Featuring contributions by Michael Palin, Clive Dunn, Ian Lavender, Jimmy Perry and Joan Le Mesurier. Narrated by Julian Rhind-Tutt.(n)

programme.title: Dad's Army
programme.desc: Walmington-on-Sea leaves its mark on a visiting French general when Mainwaring's Home Guard heroes repaint Lady Maltby's Rolls-Royce just in time for an official engagement. Comedy first broadcast in 1974, starring Arthur Lowe and John Le Mesurier.(n)

programme.title: Great Asian Railway Journeys
programme.desc: Michael Portillo travels to Java's Central Province and the royal city of Yogyakarta, where he visits the Sultan's Palace and admires the ancient art of shadow puppetry. Moving north to Ambawara, Michael boards a scenic heritage line constructed by the Dutch with carriages dating from the time of his Bradshaw's guidebook. Moving on, he learns how coffee was first brought to Java for cultivation, visits the Great Mosque and ends at Surabaya, a port bursting with local traditions, produce and culture.(n)

programme.title: Live at the Apollo
programme.sub-title: Dara Ó Briain, Zoe Lyons, Paul Chowdhry
programme.desc: Mock the Week host Dara O Briain comperes an evening of stand-up at London's Hammersmith Apollo, treating the audience to his own brand of humour, before introducing routines by comedy circuit stars Zoe Lyons and Paul Chowdhry.(n)

programme.title: The A-Z of Eurovision
programme.desc: Rylan Clark-Neal narrates this compilation of all things Eurovision, on the evening of what would have been the global singing contest's 2020 event in Rotterdam. The alphabetical guide recalls some of the greatest moments in the show's 64-year history - as well as those perhaps best forgotten. Highlights include the best interval acts, comic voting gaffes and the funniest commentary moments from Terry Wogan and Graham Norton - not to mention dancing gorillas, Polish butter churners, fainting backing singers and Celine Dion on a tractor.(n)

programme.title: TOTP2
programme.desc: Steve Wright presents a selection of archive hits from the contest in this repeat from 2007. Featuring performances by Abba, Cliff Richard, Bucks Fizz, Katrina and the Waves, Lulu and Brotherhood of Man, plus an appearance by that year's UK entrants Scooch.(n)

programme.title: The Joy of Painting
programme.desc: American artist Bob Ross offers further advice as he paints another picture. Today he creates a seascape, with crashing waves and a sky made of a mixture of gold, pink and mauve.(n)

programme.title: The Beauty of Books
programme.desc: A look at the emergence of paperback cover design in the 20th century and how it became a way of selling a book in the mass market. The programme looks at the changing designs on novels such as George Orwell's Nineteen Eighty-Four, drawing in new readers by reflecting each decade, as well as the advent of electronic readers and how novels face more scrutiny as they compete with technology.(n)

programme.title: Pride and Prejudice
programme.desc: Mrs Bennet spots an opportunity to marry off another of her daughters with the arrival of clergyman Mr Collins. However, Elizabeth is unable to take his proposal seriously, being too preoccupied with a handsome newcomer who poisons her mind further against Mr Darcy. Alison Steadman, Jennifer Ehle and David Bamber star in Andrew Davies' adaptation of Jane Austen's classic novel.(n)

programme.title: The BBC Lockdown Orchestra: You Got the Love
programme.desc: A virtual performance of the Candi Staton disco classic by musicians from all six BBC orchestras, choirs and the Ulster Orchestra - augmented by people joining in from home.(n)

programme.title: Earth from Space
programme.desc: Cameras in space tell stories of life on our planet from a brand new perspective, to reveal new discoveries and explore Earth's most remote habitats. In the first episode, satellites follow an elephant family which is struggling through a drought, reveal previously unknown penguin colonies, and discover mysterious ice rings that could put seal pups in danger.(n)

programme.title: Chasing the Moon
programme.sub-title: A Place Beyond the Sky - Part One
programme.desc: Documentary detailing America's determination to put a man on the Moon ahead of Soviet Russia, beginning with how the launch of Sputnik 1 in 1957 compelled the USA to join the space race.(n)

programme.title: Science and Islam
programme.desc: Professor Jim Al-Khalili continues his investigation into scientific methods originating from the Islamic world with a trip to Northern Syria, where he learns about Al-Biruni, the astronomer and mathematician who, 1,000 years ago, estimated the size of Earth to within a few hundred miles of the correct figure. He also visits Cairo to explore physicist Ibn al-Haytham's contribution to the modern science of optics.(n)

programme.title: The Sky at Night
programme.desc: The programme celebrates its 800th episode, with filming taking place under lockdown, as Chris Lintott explores how some astronomers are managing to continue making observations - including controlling telescopes as far away as Hawaii from a laptop in their bedrooms in Belfast.(n)

programme.title: Anjelica Huston on James Joyce: A Shout in the Street
programme.desc: Anjelica Huston tells the story of the extraordinary life and work of the literary giant, from his impoverished childhood, through worldwide lfame, to his desperate flight from the Nazis. Contributors include writers Colm Toibin, Jeffrey Eugenides, Anne Enright, John Banville, Fintan O'Toole, David Simon, Ruth Gilligan, Edna O'Brien and Eimear McBride.(n)

programme.title: Sleuths, Spies & Sorcerers: Andrew Marr's Paperback Heroes
programme.desc: The broadcaster explores the genres of fiction that are the most popular with the general public, unpicking their conventions to show why they are such page-turners. He begins by looking at detective novels, profiling pioneers Agatha Christie, Dashiell Hammett and Ruth Rendell, and decoding set-pieces such as Hercule Poirot's drawing room denouements and the locked room mysteries of John Dickson Carr. With contributions by Ian Rankin, Sophie Hannah, Anthony Horowitz and Val McDermid.(n)

programme.title: The Joy of Painting
programme.desc: American artist Bob Ross offers further advice as he paints another picture. Today he creates a seascape, with crashing waves and a sky made of a mixture of gold, pink and mauve.(n)

programme.title: Earth from Space
programme.desc: Cameras in space tell stories of life on our planet from a brand new perspective, to reveal new discoveries and explore Earth's most remote habitats. In the first episode, satellites follow an elephant family which is struggling through a drought, reveal previously unknown penguin colonies, and discover mysterious ice rings that could put seal pups in danger.(n)

programme.title: Classic Comps
programme.desc: Compilations of performances from the BBC archives, including Ravi Shankar's Raaf Bihag and Dudley Moore playing the Colonel Bogey March. Also featuring appearances by guitarist John Williams, cellist Jacqueline du Pre, trumpeter Wynton Marsalis and violinist Nigel Kennedy on programmes including Parkinson, Later with Jools Holland, The Les Dawson Show, Music at Night and Wogan.(n)

programme.title: Top of the Pops: 1989
programme.desc: Nicky Campbell and Anthea Turner present the edition originally broadcast on 17 August, featuring performances by acts including Neneh Cherry, Martika and Fuzzbox.(n)

programme.title: Kermode and Mayo's Home Entertainment Service
programme.desc: With cinemas closed and the nation on lockdown, Mark Kermode and Simon Mayo turn their attention to the small screen, presenting a guide to the best - and worst - of streaming culture, across movies and premium TV. In the first edition, they review Netflix's The Eddy, BBC's Normal People (including a chat with director Lenny Abrahamson), Eliza Hittman-directed Never Rarely Sometimes Always, Mubi docu-feature Romantic Comedy and Terry Gilliam's classic Twelve Monkeys.(n)

programme.title: Top of the Pops: 1989
programme.desc: Mark Goodier and Jakki Brambles present the edition first shown on 24 August, featuring performances by the likes of Then Jerico, Cliff Richard and Bon Jovi.(n)

programme.title: Eurovision at 60
programme.desc: Documentary looking at the song contest since its launch in 1956 up to 2015. The programme reveals how the annual event has blossomed from involving just seven countries to garnering some 195 million viewers across the continent. Hosts and competitors tell the behind-the-scenes story of Eurovision - widely regarded as the greatest and most eccentric song contest on Earth.(n)

programme.title: Dana - The Original Derry Girl
programme.desc: The story of the singer, who won the 1970 Eurovision Song Contest for Ireland with All Kinds of Everything. At a time when the violent conflict of the Troubles was dominating the news, Rosemary Scallon - better known as Dana - became a national treasure overnight while still a teenager. The programme looks at what happened after she won the competition, including her successful pop and TV career in the 70s, her marriage to Newry hotelier Damien Scallon, her move to Alabama, and her stint in Irish politics.(n)

programme.title: People's History of Pop
programme.desc: Pauline Black looks at the years 1976 to 1985, when music got involved in passionate protest and the high street filled with colourful factions of music lovers. After a lot of big hair and big rock stars, punks brought pop back down to earth, and out of that music lovers shattered into an array of pop tribes who posed with passion. The programme features an Asian punk band whose members were inspired by the music to shout for what they believed in, and includes some precious material that offers an insight into the thinking of the Clash's lead singer, Joe Strummer.(n)

programme.title: Country Music by Ken Burns
programme.desc: The history of the American art form and how it evolved across the 20th century. The first programme explores the genre's earliest years up to 1933, when so-called 'hillbilly music' grew in popularity. After centuries of percolating in the American South, hillbilly began reaching more people through the new technologies of phonographs and radio. The Carter Family, with their ballads and old hymns, and Jimmie Rodgers, with his combination of blues and yodeling, became its first big stars. Contributors include Dolly Parton, Rosanne Cash and Merle Haggard. Narrated by Peter Coyote.(n)

programme.title: Eurovision at 60
programme.desc: Documentary looking at the song contest since its launch in 1956 up to 2015. The programme reveals how the annual event has blossomed from involving just seven countries to garnering some 195 million viewers across the continent. Hosts and competitors tell the behind-the-scenes story of Eurovision - widely regarded as the greatest and most eccentric song contest on Earth.(n)

programme.title: Natural World
programme.sub-title: Natural World: Pygmy Hippo - A Very Secret Life
programme.desc: Solitary, secretive and extremely hard to find, the pygmy hippo is one of nature's last great mysteries and scientists know almost nothing about its life in the wild and what it needs to survive. This documentary follows Australian ecologist Wei-Yeen Yap as she heads to a remote part of the West African rainforest to investigate its life and habits, hoping to gain knowledge that will help save the animal from extinction. David Harewood narrates.(n)

programme.title: How the Wild West Was Won with Ray Mears
programme.desc: How the landscapes of America's five great deserts challenged the pioneers' westward push. Beginning in Monument Valley on the Arizona-Utah state line, Ray discovers how the US cavalry struggled in the hot arid landscape, explores how the Apache adapted their warfare methods to the hostile conditions and reveals why the forbidding landscape proved the perfect refuge for bandits.(n)

programme.title: State of Happiness
programme.desc: Phillips Petroleum is looking for a place by the coast to build a gigantic tank for oil storage and Dirdal Valley, where Anna's family's farm is situated, is one of the areas they are considering. What Anna learns from her work at the mayor's office has dire consequences. In Norwegian and English.(n)

programme.title: State of Happiness
programme.desc: Christian and Martin start their own diving company and are in need of start-up capital, while Ingrid and Fredrik Nyman make a plan to help their son, and an old friend of Toril's returns to town. In Norwegian and English.(n)

programme.title: Made in Dagenham (2010)
programme.desc: The female employees making upholstery at Ford's London plant in the 1960s campaign for the right to equal pay, only for the company to declare their work is unskilled. The women embark on a national protest, taking their grievance all the way to the government and demanding a change in the law. Fact-based comedy drama, starring Sally Hawkins, Miranda Richardson and Bob Hoskins.(n)

programme.title: The Real Marigold Hotel
programme.desc: The group begin their second week, having settled into their new home on the coast of Puducherry. Henry Blofeld is keen to find a cure for an old injury, so he and Paul Chuckle visit a local hospital where western medicine is combined with yoga. Paul also braves the chaotic roads as he throws himself headlong into his first Indian driving lesson, while Britt Ekland and Zandra Rhodes explore the more spiritual side of their temporary home. Later in the week, some of the group catch the sleeper train to Madurai, where they enjoy the wild celebrations of the famous Chithirai festival.(n)

programme.title: Weatherman Walking
programme.desc: Derek Brockway walks along a stunning - and often overlooked - stretch of coastline, dominated by the Second Severn Crossing or Prince of Wales Bridge. On his journey, he rings an ancient church bell, finds a water scorpion that breathes through its bottom, learns about the Rogiet Hoard and discovers how the village of Sudbrook evolved. He also wades into the River Severn, following in the footsteps of ancient fisherman, and finishes in Chepstow at the start - or end - of the Wales Coast Path.(n)

programme.title: My Family
programme.desc: Brigitte arranges a blind date for Janey with a wealthy patient's handsome son and feels obliged to deliver the birds-and-bees speech, but ends up learning a bit herself. Meanwhile, Nick pursues his own research and decides to become an artist - with the prospect of nude models in the house high on his list of priorities.(n)

programme.title: Not Going Out
programme.desc: Toby organises a sponsored parachute jump to raise money for a children's ward in his hospital, so Lee, Lucy, Anna and the grandparents all agree to take part to support him. But once airborne, will everyone pluck up the courage to jump? Return of the comedy, starring Lee Mack, Sally Bretton and Hugh Dennis.(n)

programme.title: Have I Got News for You
programme.desc: Jo Brand guest hosts the satirical current affairs quiz, with regular team captains Ian Hislop and Paul Merton joined by columnist Katy Balls and stand-up comedian Ivo Graham to poke fun at the week's events.(n)

programme.title: Peter Kay's Car Share
programme.desc: Peter Kay stars in the first episode of the award-winning sitcom about the daily commute of two supermarket employees thrown together in a company car-share scheme, with each daily trip bringing fresh insight into their lives. But will they actually get along? Things don't get off to a good start for assistant manager John when his sat-nav leads him a merry dance as he tries to find promotions rep Kayleigh's house - and when she does finally get in the car, it doesn't take long for her to mark her territory. Sian Gibson co-stars.(n)

programme.title: Pointless Celebrities
programme.desc: Alexander Armstrong and Richard Osman present as famous faces take part in a star-studded version of the general knowledge quiz in which the contestants must try to come up with the least likely correct answers to a series of questions posed to members of the public. In this edition, Jay Aston, Mike Nolan, SuRie, Dana, Jade Ewen, John Lundvik, Niamh Kavanagh and Mans Zelmerlow take part in a special Eurovision edition of the general knowledge quiz. Plus, a special performance from Dana.(n)

programme.title: Eurovision: Come Together
programme.desc: With Eurovision 2020 unable to go ahead as planned, Graham Norton gives viewers the chance to vote for their all-time favourite Eurovision song from an eclectic shortlist, selected by a panel of experts. Graham also chats to James Newman, who was due to represent the UK in Rotterdam this year. There are previews of some of the other most talked-about acts who were due to take to the stage this year, and Eurovision fans and recreate classic moments in their homes. Former UK act Sonia even takes on the Epic Sax Guy solo that became so infamous after the 2010 Eurovision Song Contest.(n)

programme.title: Eurovision: Europe Shine a Light
programme.desc: A special programme honouring all 41 songs that would have competed in the 2020 Eurovision song contest. Just like the contest itself, the live show will be simultaneously broadcast across Europe and beyond, and will be hosted from the Netherlands by Dutch presenters Chantal Janzen, Edsilia Rombley and Jan Smit. There will be a virtual singalong, where fans from across the world will dress up and form a huge Eurovision choir to perform Johnny Logan's winning song for Ireland in 1980, What's Another Year. Graham Norton provides live commentary.(n)

programme.title: Landward
programme.desc: Euan McIlwraith learns about a striking new discovery at the Calanais stone circle on Lewis and Anne Lundon finds out about the transformation of the River Kelvin. Arlene Stuart meets red kites and Dougie Vipond finds out how the fishing industry has been hit by lockdown.(n)

programme.title: My Family
programme.desc: Brigitte arranges a blind date for Janey with a wealthy patient's handsome son and feels obliged to deliver the birds-and-bees speech, but ends up learning a bit herself. Meanwhile, Nick pursues his own research and decides to become an artist - with the prospect of nude models in the house high on his list of priorities.(n)

programme.title: Not Going Out
programme.desc: Toby organises a sponsored parachute jump to raise money for a children's ward in his hospital, so Lee, Lucy, Anna and the grandparents all agree to take part to support him. But once airborne, will everyone pluck up the courage to jump? Return of the comedy, starring Lee Mack, Sally Bretton and Hugh Dennis.(n)

programme.title: Have I Got News for You
programme.desc: Jo Brand guest hosts the satirical current affairs quiz, with regular team captains Ian Hislop and Paul Merton joined by columnist Katy Balls and stand-up comedian Ivo Graham to poke fun at the week's events.(n)

programme.title: Peter Kay's Car Share
programme.desc: Peter Kay stars in the first episode of the award-winning sitcom about the daily commute of two supermarket employees thrown together in a company car-share scheme, with each daily trip bringing fresh insight into their lives. But will they actually get along? Things don't get off to a good start for assistant manager John when his sat-nav leads him a merry dance as he tries to find promotions rep Kayleigh's house - and when she does finally get in the car, it doesn't take long for her to mark her territory. Sian Gibson co-stars.(n)

programme.title: Pointless Celebrities
programme.desc: Alexander Armstrong and Richard Osman present as famous faces take part in a star-studded version of the general knowledge quiz in which the contestants must try to come up with the least likely correct answers to a series of questions posed to members of the public. In this edition, Jay Aston, Mike Nolan, SuRie, Dana, Jade Ewen, John Lundvik, Niamh Kavanagh and Mans Zelmerlow take part in a special Eurovision edition of the general knowledge quiz. Plus, a special performance from Dana.(n)

programme.title: Eurovision: Come Together
programme.desc: With Eurovision 2020 unable to go ahead as planned, Graham Norton gives viewers the chance to vote for their all-time favourite Eurovision song from an eclectic shortlist, selected by a panel of experts. Graham also chats to James Newman, who was due to represent the UK in Rotterdam this year. There are previews of some of the other most talked-about acts who were due to take to the stage this year, and Eurovision fans and recreate classic moments in their homes. Former UK act Sonia even takes on the Epic Sax Guy solo that became so infamous after the 2010 Eurovision Song Contest.(n)

programme.title: Eurovision: Europe Shine a Light
programme.desc: A special programme honouring all 41 songs that would have competed in the 2020 Eurovision song contest. Just like the contest itself, the live show will be simultaneously broadcast across Europe and beyond, and will be hosted from the Netherlands by Dutch presenters Chantal Janzen, Edsilia Rombley and Jan Smit. There will be a virtual singalong, where fans from across the world will dress up and form a huge Eurovision choir to perform Johnny Logan's winning song for Ireland in 1980, What's Another Year. Graham Norton provides live commentary.(n)

programme.title: DIY SOS
programme.desc: Nick Knowles and the team return to Veterans Street in Manchester, where, in 2015, with the help of Prince William and Prince Harry, DIY SOS launched their most ambitious project yet - transforming a derelict street into a vibrant community for veterans and local people. This time they build the final home for decorated former soldier and single dad Simon Flores, whose foot was blown off by a roadside bomb during a patrol in Iraq.(n)

programme.title: The Real Marigold Hotel
programme.desc: The group begin their second week, having settled into their new home on the coast of Puducherry. Henry Blofeld is keen to find a cure for an old injury, so he and Paul Chuckle visit a local hospital where western medicine is combined with yoga. Paul also braves the chaotic roads as he throws himself headlong into his first Indian driving lesson, while Britt Ekland and Zandra Rhodes explore the more spiritual side of their temporary home. Later in the week, some of the group catch the sleeper train to Madurai, where they enjoy the wild celebrations of the famous Chithirai festival.(n)

programme.title: 1964 General Election
programme.desc: A chance to revisit the BBC's coverage of the historic General Election of 1964, which saw Harold Wilson's Labour Party secure a majority of just four seats to form what would eventually turn out to be an unstable and unworkable parliament. Richard Dimbleby presents as live as the results were delivered on Thursday, October 15. Includes analysis by Ian Trethowan, David Butler and Robert McKenzie and interviews by Robin Day, Cliff Michelmore and David Frost.(n)

programme.title: Panorama
programme.sub-title: Lockdown: How Business Is Coping - Panorama
programme.desc: Britain's economy has been turned upside down by the coronavirus crisis. Many companies are struggling to stay afloat, while some have found themselves swamped with unprecedented demand. Panorama follows some of the small business owners and key workers struggling through the lockdown - from the courier whose job it is to collect suspected samples of Covid-19, to the funeral director working round the clock to collect and bury the dead.(n)

programme.title: Coronation Street
programme.desc: Oliver suffers another frightening seizure and is moved to intensive care, much to Steve and Leanne's horror. Ed and Michael experience racism at work, while Gemma attends a support group for her depression and takes comfort from realising other people are in the same position as her.(n)

programme.title: Birds of a Feather
programme.desc: As the comedy returns, there's bad news for the Stubbs family as they hear that Tracey's ex-husband has died and she has to decide what kind of send-off to give him. Sharon wants it to be nice for the boys, Chigwell's criminal fraternity are keen to make it special, while Dorien seems more interested in just finding the right outfit. Linda Robson, Pauline Quirke and Lesley Joseph star, with appearances from Spandau Ballet's Martin Kemp and one-time EastEnder Jamie Foreman (Derek Branning).(n)

programme.title: Birds of a Feather
programme.desc: The family are devastated when unscrupulous burglars take advantage of their absence at Darryl's funeral to ransack the house. But rather than involve the Old Bill, they decide to track down their possessions themselves, setting out on a trail that takes them from a snooker parlour and a pawnbroker's to an infamous local drag club. Comedy, starring Pauline Quirke and Linda Robson.(n)

programme.title: Mr Magoo

programme.title: Mighty Mike
programme.desc: Mike's over the moon! He's going to spend the afternoon with the neighbor's dog. Except that it's not Iris, it's Ginette.(n)

programme.title: Mighty Mike

programme.title: Mr Bean: The Animated Series
programme.desc: Bean's landlady orders him to get rid of a mole making a mess of her exquisitely manicured croquet lawn.(n)

programme.title: Mr Bean: The Animated Series
programme.desc: The clumsy hero is kept awake by the deafening sound of roadworks outside his bedroom window, but a plot to restore peace by feeding the workmen goes spectacularly wrong.(n)

programme.title: Ben 10
programme.desc: Ben hops into the Rustbuggy for a driving lesson along the sand dunes of Cape Cod.(n)

programme.sub-title: Captain Underpants and the Jarring Jerkiness of the Judge J.O.R.T.S
programme.desc: The boys' jorts-wearing Spanish teacher is inspired by Harold and George to become a superhero.(n)

programme.title: The Rubbish World of Dave Spud
programme.desc: Dave's first plane journey doesn't go to plan when the air pressure and the need to go to the toilet makes his head swell.(n)

programme.title: The Rubbish World of Dave Spud
programme.desc: Instructed to keep an eye on Gran while the rest of the Spuds visit to the doctor, Dave finds that she has escaped.(n)

programme.title: James Martin's Saturday Morning
programme.desc: The TV chef presents more highlights from the series, including recipes from Thomasina Miers, Nisha Katona and Jonathan Phang, and a meal prepared for actor Larry Lamb.(n)

programme.title: James Martin's Islands to Highlands
programme.desc: On the next leg of his Islands to Highlands tour, James travels to Devon where he meets his friend and Michelin-starred chef Simon Hulstone. While they are in the area, James visits a mushroom farm, and Simon samples some award-winning cheese, which inspires James to prepare an extravagant cheese platter.(n)

programme.title: Ainsley's Mediterranean Cookbook
programme.desc: Ainsley Harriott's culinary tour reaches Granada in southern Spain, where he learns about the famous Alhambra Palace, enjoys a lesson in flamenco dancing and visits the caves of Sacromante, home to a cooking school where he is taught to make classic tortilla and fried aubergines. Ainsley also samples Spanish hot chocolate - thick, creamy and flavoured with orange - and tries local delicacy piononos, with tour guide Katia. Along the way, he rustles up chicken with saffron potatoes and a classic seafood paella.(n)

programme.title: Love Your Garden
programme.desc: Alan Titchmarsh and the team head for rural Cornwall, where they create a spectacular contemporary garden for Chris Finney, a former serviceman who was awarded the George Cross in 2003. A space age-inspired area - complete with solar dome - will test Chris's knowledge as a novice gardener, and provide a stimulating haven for his young family.(n)

programme.title: Dickinson's Real Deal

programme.title: Britain's Got Talent
programme.desc: Ant and Dec host the 14th series of nationwide talent search, which sees a wide variety of acts, from comedians to contortionists, singers to spoon players, compete for the chance to win £250,000 and a coveted slot at the Royal Variety Performance. Simon Cowell, Amanda Holden, Alesha Dixon and David Walliams are once again on hand to judge this week's applicants, however, they have all used up their Golden Buzzers already - each granting a place in the live semi-finals to their chosen acts.(n)

programme.title: Tomorrow Never Dies (1997)

programme.title: Heartbeat
programme.desc: Joe and George call a meeting of local farmers to bring to an end their harassment of a neighbour whose unacceptable farming practices have made him deeply unpopular. Guest starring Phyllis Logan and Tim Brooke-Taylor.(n)

programme.title: Man About the House
programme.desc: Chrissy and Robin spend an evening at home alone - and the absence of a chaperone allows the trainee chef's amorous side to surface. Comedy, starring Richard O'Sullivan.(n)

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Marple
programme.desc: Lady Virginia Revel receives a marriage proposal from an ambitious politician, but is reluctant to accept it, especially as she is also being courted by a dashing young suitor. Accompanied by Miss Marple, she travels to a weekend party at her family home Chimneys, hoping to reach a decision. Negotiations for the sale of the country house are taking place during the gathering, but when new owner Count Ludwig of Austria is found murdered, St Mary Mead's elderly spinster must identify the culprit. Whodunit, starring Julia McKenzie, with a supporting cast including Gavin & Stacey stars Ruth Jones and Mathew Horne, plus Dervla Kirwan, Charlotte Salt, Michelle Collins, Jonas Armstrong and Edward Fox.(n)

programme.title: Heartbeat
programme.desc: Armed robbers target post offices around Aidensfield and Mason finds himself working with old flame Rachel Dawson, whose career is under threat following an affair with a senior colleague. Carol is not impressed when Rachel is spotted in a clinch with Joe, but he claims it was in the line of duty and completely innocent. Peggy offers to clean for Edward Walton (played by former Likely Lads actor Rodney Bewes), raising Oscar's suspicion she is after his money, but it is Edward's wife, now in a nursing home, who turns out to have a shady past.(n)

programme.desc: Pete and Frank set up a hidden camera at Home Farm to monitor Kim. Chris has a business proposal for Jack and Sarah. The dance competition gets under way.(n)

programme.title: Classic Emmerdale

programme.desc: The Duckworths plan how to spend their money. Liz thinks she's pregnant. Curly worries when Reg and Eric have exclusive meetings without him.(n)

programme.desc: The McDonalds struggle with their feelings about Liz's pregnancy. Jack and Vera make plans to buy the shop. The Websters agree that Sally should tell Bill he has to move out.(n)

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Marple
programme.desc: Millionaire's wife Mary Pritchard, a notoriously demanding invalid, apparently dies of shock in her locked room when the geranium in her wallpaper turns blue, fulfilling the dire warning of a mysterious fortune teller. The manner of her death creates a sensation in the tense village community of Little Ambrose, but although the woman's husband confesses to the crime, Miss Marple uncovers new evidence that could affect the court hearing's outcome and races to halt proceedings before the wrong person is convicted. Starring Julia McKenzie, with Toby Stephens, Sharon Small, Patrick Baladi, Caroline Catz, Claire Rushbrook and Donald Sinden.(n)

programme.title: Heartbeat
programme.desc: Armed robbers target post offices around Aidensfield and Mason finds himself working with old flame Rachel Dawson, whose career is under threat following an affair with a senior colleague. Carol is not impressed when Rachel is spotted in a clinch with Joe, but he claims it was in the line of duty and completely innocent. programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.desc: Wealthy industrialist Sir Reuben Astwell is murdered after making a deal to sell synthetic rubber to the Germans, leaving the dapper sleuth wondering whether the motive was commercial or political. Evidence found in his chemical factory could hold the key to the mystery - but Poirot is unsure if the victim's widow can be trusted. Starring David Suchet, with Ann Bell and Bill Wallis.(n)

programme.title: Midsomer Murders
programme.desc: DCI Tom Barnaby reluctantly joins wife Joyce on a spa weekend at Swavely Manor, a country house offering treatments to soothe the mind and body. However, as he attempts to unwind, a woman is found dead in a flotation chamber and he promptly abandons his therapy to investigate - but the body count soon rises. The detective eventually wraps up the case, but it proves to be his last as he makes a big decision about his future. John Nettles' final appearance in the long-running series, with guest stars Ronni Ancona, Geraldine James and Lesley Manville.(n)

programme.title: Bancroft
programme.desc: Katherine and Anya make a determined push to get to the bottom of the Laura Fraser case once and for all, hatching a plan to get the DNA evidence they desperately need. Bancroft executes a massive sting operation against Athif Kamara, but things do not run smoothly and she is forced to reveal carefully kept secrets about her past. Thriller, starring Sarah Parish, Faye Marsay and Amara Khan.(n)

programme.title: Inspector Morse
programme.desc: A woman is found murdered near a driving school and the investigation proves a testing time for Morse and especially Lewis, who has the added distraction of being paired with an attractive female colleague. Detective drama, starring John Thaw and Kevin Whately, with Patrick Malahide, Tessa Wojtczak and Christopher Fulford.(n)

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.sub-title: The Mystery of the Spanish Chest
programme.desc: A lavish dinner party proves to be a prelude to murder and intrigue - but fortunately Poirot is among the guests, and soon realises the key to the mystery lies in a duel fought 10 years previously. Drama, starring David Suchet, Caroline Langrishe and John McEnery.(n)

programme.desc: The McDonalds struggle with their feelings about Liz's pregnancy. Jack and Vera make plans to buy the shop. The Websters agree that Sally should tell Bill he has to move out.(n)

programme.title: Classic Emmerdale
programme.desc: Pete and Frank set up a hidden camera at Home Farm to monitor Kim. Chris has a business proposal for Jack and Sarah. The dance competition gets under way.(n)

programme.title: Classic Emmerdale
programme.desc: Ned and Jan confront Dave about the affair. Seth gives Biff some marriage advice. Vic and Viv discover Scott's gun and attempt to wrestle it off him, but the trigger goes off.(n)

programme.title: Heartbeat
programme.desc: Armed robbers target post offices around Aidensfield and Mason finds himself working with old flame Rachel Dawson, whose career is under threat following an affair with a senior colleague. Carol is not impressed when Rachel is spotted in a clinch with Joe, but he claims it was in the line of duty and completely innocent. Peggy offers to clean for Edward Walton (played by former Likely Lads actor Rodney Bewes), raising Oscar's suspicion she is after his money, but it is Edward's wife, now in a nursing home, who turns out to have a shady past.(n)

programme.title: The Darling Buds of May
programme.sub-title: Cast Not Your Pearls Before Swine - Part Two
programme.desc: Part two of two. Roger returns to Liverpool, while Pop discovers Edith's new boyfriend George is a con-artist defrauding the ministry - and resolves to teach him a lesson. David Jason stars.(n)

programme.title: Man About the House
programme.desc: Robin comes home with a puppy, to Chrissy's dismay. Flat-share comedy, with Richard O'Sullivan, Sally Thomsett and Paula Wilcox.(n)

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Marple
programme.desc: Millionaire's wife Mary Pritchard, a notoriously demanding invalid, apparently dies of shock in her locked room when the geranium in her wallpaper turns blue, fulfilling the dire warning of a mysterious fortune teller. The manner of her death creates a sensation in the tense village community of Little Ambrose, but although the woman's husband confesses to the crime, Miss Marple uncovers new evidence that could affect the court hearing's outcome and races to halt proceedings before the wrong person is convicted. Starring Julia McKenzie, with Toby Stephens, Sharon Small, Patrick Baladi, Caroline Catz, Claire Rushbrook and Donald Sinden.(n)

programme.title: Heartbeat
programme.desc: Eileen Needham sues for divorce after a domestic dispute gets out of hand, and her hard-nosed solicitor Sylvia Swinton (played by Outnumbered actress Samantha Bond) does little to calm the situation, so Joe steps in to play the peacemaker. Stan Bickle arrives in Aidensfield, bringing his daughter Josie (guest star Jenna Boyd), whose flirtatious ways steal David's heart and bring out the worst in Peggy.(n)

programme.title: Classic Emmerdale
programme.desc: The police arrive at the Windsors to find Scott still missing. Rachel gives Chris an ultimatum. Nick tells Pete the whole story about Kim's threat to Alice.(n)

programme.title: Classic Emmerdale
programme.desc: Frank is set on revenge and Rachel reaches the end of the line with Chris.(n)

programme.title: Classic Coronation Street
programme.desc: Jack has second thoughts about buying the shop. Liz is delighted when Steve phones to say he and Vicky are married. Reg is stunned when Eric makes a big announcement.(n)

programme.title: Classic Coronation Street
programme.desc: Jack decides to throw a party for his neighbours. Percy commemorates VJ Day in the Rovers. Reg attempts to convince the Duckworths to think again about buying the shop.(n)

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Marple
programme.desc: The sleuth helps an old friend find refuge at country house Greenshaw's Folly, but a sinister shadow falls over the strange building's various inhabitants and a murderer strikes. Miss Marple uncovers secrets from the past about Miss Greenshaw as a dangerous storm gathers and the bodies start to pile up. Julia McKenzie heads an all-star cast that includes Jim Moir (better known as Vice Reeves), Kimberley Nixon, Fiona Shaw, Robert Glenister, Julia Sawalha, Judy Parfitt and Matt Willis (formerly of pop group Busted).(n)

programme.title: Heartbeat
programme.desc: Eileen Needham sues for divorce after a domestic dispute gets out of hand, and her hard-nosed solicitor Sylvia Swinton (played by Outnumbered actress Samantha Bond) does little to calm the situation, so Joe steps in to play the peacemaker. Stan Bickle arrives in Aidensfield, bringing his daughter Josie (guest star Jenna Boyd), whose flirtatious ways steal David's heart and bring out the worst in Peggy.(n)

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.desc: A mysterious delivery causes Poirot to recall the time his supper at a Buenos Aires restaurant was rudely interrupted by the murder of a fellow diner, who unwittingly drank from a glass laced with cyanide. Drama, starring David Suchet and Philip Jackson.(n)

programme.title: Doc Martin
programme.desc: Mark has an unusual idea for a stag night, which involves camping under the stars rather than going to the pub. However, the jaunt ends in disaster when he becomes lost in the woods and is bitten by a snake, leaving Martin with the task of locating the clueless copper and saving his marriage before it has even begun. Meanwhile, the teenagers of Portwenn provoke the doctor's wrath by inventing a deadly game that threatens one boy's life. Martin Clunes stars.(n)

programme.title: Doc Martin
programme.desc: Mark is forced to investigate his fiancee's past when a stranger arrives in the village with a mysterious photo. Mrs Tishell finally removes the neck collar she has worn for years, and suffers a prolapsed disc as a result. Martin reveals his true feelings for Louisa over a few glasses of wine, but he drinks too much and wakes the next morning to a waiting room full of giggling patients, a raging hangover and a distinct feeling of uneasiness.(n)

programme.title: Lewis
programme.desc: The detective suspects a connection between the death of a student with secret plans for advancement and the killing of well-known criminal Nicky Turnball, who was in Oxford to speak at the Union. As Lewis and Hathaway eliminate suspects from a long list of the crook's enemies, the investigation brings him into contact with old flame Diane - who also happens to be Turnball's wife. Kevin Whately stars, with Laurence Fox and Gina McKee.(n)

programme.title: Inspector Morse
programme.desc: A rehearsal of an amateur operatic production ends with Morse being arrested on suspicion of murder and suspended from duty pending further investigation. Stripped of official powers, he fights to clear his name - unaware he has been pencilled in as the real killer's next target. Guest starring Ian McDiarmid, with John Thaw and Kevin Whately.(n)

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.sub-title: The Affair at the Victory Ball
programme.desc: The sleuth is invited to attend a masquerade ball dressed as someone famous and modestly goes as himself - and finds time amid the revelry to solve the murder of aristocratic fellow guest Lord Cronshaw. David Suchet stars.(n)

programme.title: ITV3 Nightscreen
programme.desc: Text-based information service.(n)

programme.title: Teleshopping
programme.desc: Buying goods from the comfort of home.(n)

programme.title: Man About the House
programme.desc: The rent money disappears, forcing Jo to consider selling a prized possession. Flat-share comedy, with Sally Thomsett, Paula Wilcox and Richard O'Sullivan.(n)

programme.title: Love Your Garden
programme.desc: Alan Titchmarsh and his team come to the aid of nonagenarian Roberta and her 86-year-old best friend and carer Enid, who live together in a retirement bungalow in Wiveliscombe, Somerset. Originally from Liverpool, the two plucky pensioners have helped each other out countless times throughout the course of their friendship, which has lasted seven decades. However, they now find themselves trapped in their home by a treacherous slope in their garden, so Alan plans to set them free by designing a future-proofed garden in a vintage style.(n)

programme.title: Love Your Garden
programme.desc: Alan Titchmarsh and the team are in Dorset creating an exciting garden for a young family. His idea for the Drakes is to give them an outdoor space that reflects terminally ill dad Andrew's love of the great outdoors and his passion for working with wood. He opts for a garden with a frontier-land theme that Andrew, wife Rachel and their young son Lucas can enjoy, creating a prairie-inspired back yard of big scale rustic wood structures, agricultural motifs with craftsmanship and the spirit of adventure at its core.(n)

programme.title: Coronation Street
programme.desc: Steve and Leanne view a nursery school for Oliver, but matters take a dramatic turn on their return home when Oliver suffers a seizure. Gail tells Sarah and David she expects them at her birthday soirée at Number 8, and Cathy threatens to quit her job.(n)

programme.title: Coronation Street
programme.desc: David encounters some drug dealers on a rough estate, but when he jumps on the bonnet of their car, they give chase, putting him in danger. Steve and Leanne describe Oliver's symptoms to a doctor and Cathy and Bernie call a truce at the kebab shop.(n)

programme.title: Coronation Street
programme.desc: Oliver suffers another frightening seizure and is moved to intensive care, much to Steve and Leanne's horror. Ed and Michael experience racism at work, while Gemma attends a support group for her depression and takes comfort from realising other people are in the same position as her.(n)

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.desc: When Hastings and a reluctant Poirot attend a grouse shoot at a friend's estate, one of the hunting party is murdered. Despite suffering a severe cold, the Belgian sleuth is roused from his sickbed to solve the case. David Suchet stars.(n)

programme.title: Inspector Morse
programme.desc: A diary and a parking ticket are found at a murder scene in a multi-storey car park. It is not much to go on, but Morse soon links the clues to a crime of passion involving a jealous husband, his wife and her lover. On the personal front, the Oxford detective's feathers are ruffled by a woman he finds himself attracted to. John Thaw, Kevin Whately, Mel Martin and Amanda Hillwood star.(n)

programme.title: Carry On Matron (1972)
programme.desc: A wily thief gathers a band of crooks for a plot to break into a maternity hospital and steal the supply of contraceptive pills. One of the gang gets the lie of the land by disguising himself as a nurse, but the hypochondriac head doctor and formidable matron prove constant obstacles to the heist. Comedy, starring Sid James, Kenneth Williams and Hattie Jacques.(n)

programme.title: Inspector Morse
programme.desc: A highly respected scientist is murdered just before he is about to deliver a controversial lecture on the environment. It becomes clear he had many enemies within the profession, but Morse's investigations lead him to a killer who is altogether closer to home. Mystery, starring John Thaw, Kevin Whately, Geoffrey Palmer, Cheryl Campbell and George Costigan.(n)

programme.title: Midsomer Murders
programme.desc: DCI Tom Barnaby reluctantly joins wife Joyce on a spa weekend at Swavely Manor, a country house offering treatments to soothe the mind and body. However, as he attempts to unwind, a woman is found dead in a flotation chamber and he promptly abandons his therapy to investigate - but the body count soon rises. The detective eventually wraps up the case, but it proves to be his last as he makes a big decision about his future. John Nettles' final appearance in the long-running series, with guest stars Ronni Ancona, Geraldine James and Lesley Manville.(n)

programme.title: Lewis
programme.desc: Detective drama, starring Kevin Whately as the senior detective with Laurence Fox as his sidekick Hathaway.(n)

programme.title: Midsomer Murders
programme.desc: The bodies of a glamorous senior citizen and a distinguished doctor are found beside a river - and although suspicion for their murders initially falls on poachers, Barnaby suspects a more monetary motive for the attack. However, the case becomes complicated with the discovery that a local restaurateur, who had recently learnt his wife had been having an affair with the male victim, has also met an untimely end. With Honor Blackman, Philip Franks, James Hazeldine and Jeff Rawle.(n)

programme.title: Inspector Morse
programme.desc: A rehearsal of an amateur operatic production ends with Morse being arrested on suspicion of murder and suspended from duty pending further investigation. Stripped of official powers, he fights to clear his name - unaware he has been pencilled in as the real killer's next target. Guest starring Ian McDiarmid, with John Thaw and Kevin Whately.(n)

programme.title: Teleshopping
programme.desc: Buying goods from the comfort of home.(n)

programme.title: Classic Coronation Street
programme.desc: Derek is determined to catch whoever is harassing him. Kevin is uncomfortable with Bill spending money on the family. Deirdre calls the police out when she hears a prowler.(n)

programme.title: Classic Coronation Street
programme.desc: Deirdre puts Jamie up when Tricia doesn't return home. The Duckworths can't sleep for worrying about money. Rita tells Bill he might be getting in the way at the Websters.(n)

programme.title: Classic Emmerdale
programme.desc: Zak is still trying to come to terms with Nellie's departure. Chris is determined not to let Kim win. Tina asks Terry if there is any work available at the Woolpack.(n)

programme.title: Classic Emmerdale
programme.desc: A mystery figure takes photos of Kim and Dave together. Frank arrives home from hospital. Biff and Linda get engaged but decide not to tell anyone.(n)

programme.title: Heartbeat
programme.desc: Joe and George call a meeting of local farmers to bring to an end their harassment of a neighbour whose unacceptable farming practices have made him deeply unpopular. Dawn falls for good-looking newcomer Billy Garrett, but discovers his charming manner conceals a tortured past that produces sudden angry outbursts. David is hired to help Peggy compile an inventory of house contents of a recently deceased woman, but fears his companion's dodgy dealings have upset the restless spirit of the former resident. Guest starring Phyllis Logan and Tim Brooke-Taylor.(n)

programme.title: The Darling Buds of May
programme.sub-title: Cast Not Your Pearls Before Swine - Part One
programme.desc: Part one of two. Pop plans to build a swimming pool in the shape of Ma for her birthday surprise, while Charley and Mariette take a well-earned break in France. Starring David Jason and Catherine Zeta-Jones.(n)

programme.title: Man About the House
programme.desc: Chrissy and Robin spend an evening at home alone - and the absence of a chaperone allows the trainee chef's amorous side to surface. Comedy, starring Richard O'Sullivan.(n)

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Marple
programme.desc: Lady Virginia Revel receives a marriage proposal from an ambitious politician, but is reluctant to accept it, especially as she is also being courted by a dashing young suitor. Accompanied by Miss Marple, she travels to a weekend party at her family home Chimneys, hoping to reach a decision. Negotiations for the sale of the country house are taking place during the gathering, but when new owner Count Ludwig of Austria is found murdered, St Mary Mead's elderly spinster must identify the culprit. Whodunit, starring Julia McKenzie, with a supporting cast including Gavin & Stacey stars Ruth Jones and Mathew Horne, plus Dervla Kirwan, Charlotte Salt, Michelle Collins, Jonas Armstrong and Edward Fox.(n)

programme.title: Heartbeat
programme.desc: Armed robbers target post offices around Aidensfield and Mason finds himself working with old flame Rachel Dawson, whose career is under threat following an affair with a senior colleague. Peggy offers to clean for Edward Walton (played by former Likely Lads actor Rodney Bewes), raising Oscar's suspicion she is after his money, but it is Edward's wife, now in a nursing home, who turns out to have a shady past.(n)

programme.desc: Pete and Frank set up a hidden camera at Home Farm to monitor Kim. Chris has a business proposal for Jack and Sarah. The dance competition gets under way.(n)

programme.desc: Ned and Jan confront Dave about the affair. Seth gives Biff some marriage advice. Vic and Viv discover Scott's gun and attempt to wrestle it off him, but the trigger goes off.(n)

programme.desc: The Duckworths plan how to spend their money. Liz thinks she's pregnant. Curly worries when Reg and Eric have exclusive meetings without him.(n)

programme.desc: The McDonalds struggle with their feelings about Liz's pregnancy. Jack and Vera make plans to buy the shop. The Websters agree that Sally should tell Bill he has to move out.(n)

programme.title: Heartbeat
programme.desc: Armed robbers target post offices around Aidensfield and Mason finds himself working with old flame Rachel Dawson, whose career is under threat following an affair with a senior colleague.
